skill.name: x-article-publisher-skill
skill.summary: A skill for publishing articles to X/Twitter platform
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 4 potential patterns: 2 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags and 2 low-severity 'hardcoded URL' flags. Upon evaluation, the 'cryptographic' findings are false positives caused by GitHub HTTPS URLs in metadata, not actual encryption code. The 'hardcoded URLs' are legitimate source attribution links (lines 4, 22) with no security implications. This skill contains only documentation with no executable code, making it safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: GitHub URLs present in metadata (line 4) and documentation link (line 22). These are legitimate source attribution and documentation references with no security implications. No user input or data transmission occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are clearly for source repository attribution and documentation - standard practice in open source projects

content.value_statement: This skill helps you publish articles directly to X/Twitter. It provides guidance and patterns for efficient social media content distribution.
actual_capabilities: Provides guidance for publishing articles to X/Twitter
Offers patterns for social media content distribution
Supports workflow automation for Twitter publishing
Integrates with Claude Code for content management
Helps structure articles for Twitter format
limitations: Requires X/Twitter API credentials for actual publishing
Twitter character limits apply to published content
Rate limiting may affect publishing frequency
Network connectivity required for social media integration

use_cases.title: Content Marketer Automation
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals can automate article publication to Twitter from their content management workflow
use_cases.target_user: Content marketers and social media managers

use_cases.title: Developer Blog Sharing
use_cases.description: Developers can automatically share blog posts and technical articles to Twitter audience

use_cases.title: News Distribution
use_cases.description: Journalists and publishers can distribute articles across social media platforms efficiently

prompt_templates.title: Basic Article Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish my article to X/Twitter with a summary link
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick publishing of completed articles to social media

prompt_templates.title: Custom Tweet Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this article for Twitter with hashtags and mentions included
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating engaging tweets with proper social media formatting

prompt_templates.title: Batch Publishing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me publish these 3 articles to X/Twitter with optimal scheduling
prompt_templates.scenario: Managing multiple articles for social media distribution

prompt_templates.title: Article to Thread Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this long article into a Twitter thread for better engagement
prompt_templates.scenario: Breaking down lengthy content into threaded tweets

output_examples.input: Publish my latest blog post about AI to Twitter
output_examples.output: Article successfully published to X/Twitter with link, hashtags, and engaging summary

output_examples.input: Create a tweet thread from this article
output_examples.output: Thread created with 5 tweets, maintaining article flow and key points

best_practices: Always preview generated tweets before publishing to ensure accuracy
Include relevant hashtags to increase discoverability of your articles
Keep tweet summaries under 280 characters for optimal engagement
anti_patterns: Do not publish entire articles as single tweets - use thread format or summary with link
Avoid publishing without reviewing generated content for accuracy and tone
Do not flood Twitter with rapid-fire publications - space out your posts for better engagement

faq.question: Does this skill require X/Twitter API credentials?
faq.answer: Yes, you need valid X/Twitter API credentials to actually publish content. This skill provides the workflow and patterns for integration.

faq.question: Can I publish articles automatically without review?
faq.answer: While automation is possible, it is recommended to preview tweets before publishing to ensure accuracy and appropriate tone.

faq.question: What is the character limit for published content?
faq.answer: X/Twitter has a 280 character limit per tweet. For longer articles, use the thread format to break content into multiple tweets.

faq.question: Can this skill schedule tweets for future publishing?
faq.answer: This skill provides publishing patterns. Scheduling functionality depends on your X/Twitter API integration and tools.

faq.question: Does this work with both X and Twitter?
faq.answer: Yes, X and Twitter refer to the same platform. This skill works with the current platform API.

faq.question: Can I add images or media to published articles?
faq.answer: Media support depends on your API implementation. This skill focuses on text-based article publishing workflows.
